justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified (generates new ID, severs trace chain). NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific call paths. Consequence explicitly named: engineer must manually correlate timestamps across 3+ independent log streams with no shared identifier. Cross-service SLO gap identified (no order-placed-to-notification-sent latency metric). Specific fix recommendations provided for each trace break (extract from Kafka headers, propagate X-Trace-ID on all HTTP calls). Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally detailed component interaction map showing exactly where each trace break occurs with correct directionality
Identifies the two disconnected trace trees consequence — API Gateway→OrderService vs N orphan traces
Names the on-call debugging impact explicitly: 3am engineer must manually correlate timestamps across independent log streams
Identifies cross-service SLO gap and recommends specific metric (order_created_to_notification_sent_seconds)
Goes beyond requirements by identifying additional gaps: Kafka consumer lag metric, orphaned trace detection metric, DLQ correlation impact
Correctly identifies that OrderService→AuditService also fails to propagate trace ID despite having it available
Resilience assessment ties failure scenarios back to observability gaps rather than treating them independently
